summary: Iteration 3 e2e + backend regression for PrarthnaOS. All critical iter-3 features verified live on the production preview URL: (a) Window close-X actually removes the window from the DOM AND from the taskbar via a real `page.click()` — pointer-capture regression from iter-2 is FIXED (buttons now live outside the drag handle in Window.jsx). Verified for window-close-computer, window-close-music, window-close-book. (b) No 'Maximum update depth exceeded' or any other console errors across opening/closing 5+ windows, reload, theme toggle (collected 0 console errors / 0 pageerrors). (c) DesktopWidgets rendered at 1920×900: widget-clock shows HH:MM:SS (regex \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} matched on '17:02:59'), widget-calendar today highlighted, widget-sticky present, widget-nowplaying idle shows 'open playlist'. (d) Music app: icon labeled 'music', window TITLE 'i am... · music', body header begins 'i am...', tracklist exactly 4 (resilient, patient, hardworking, team-player). Clicking music-play → 'PLAYING (chiptune)' status, eq bars animate, widget-nowplaying updates to '● ON' + track title + widget-stop button. music-stop → 'STOPPED' + widget reverts to '■ off / open playlist'. (e) music-youtube + music-spotify anchors both target=_blank with sensible search hrefs. (f) Theme toggle taskbar-theme flips light↔dark, label ☀ pastel ↔ ☾ midnight, persists via localStorage 'prarthnaos-theme' and survives full page reload. (g) ThinkingCharacter bubble updates to mention 'i am... · music'. (h) Contact form POST → contact-success visible. (i) Backend pytest 6/6 still passing (root, contact valid, contact 422 invalid email, contact 422 missing fields, guestbook post+get, guestbook 422).

issues: Auto-opened My Computer (x:160,y:40) still overlaps the first 2-3 left-column icons — carry-over from iter1/iter2 polish item, not blocking.

critical_code_review_comments: Window.jsx: pointer-capture bug from iter2 is fixed — buttons sit in their own flex container outside the drag span with onPointerDown=stopPropagation, type=button, and per-button onClick. Real-user-equivalent Playwright clicks now succeed.
Desktop.jsx: auto-open useEffect is now guarded by autoOpenedRef.current — confirmed no infinite loop and no 'Maximum update depth exceeded' across the full QA flow (0 console errors).

Minor: DressUp testids use the outfit id (outfit-y2k / outfit-tech / outfit-summer / outfit-arcade / outfit-denim), NOT outfit-0..4. The original iter2 context note 'outfit-{0..4}' was incorrect — please update the spec / context to match the actual IDs so future automation doesn't waste a wait.

Minor: server.py still references `logger` inside submit_contact (line 93) before `logger` is defined at module bottom (line 129). Works at runtime because handler executes after import, but Pylint/Flake8 will flag and it hurts readability — move `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` to top of file.
Minor: NowPlayingMini widget reads 'now playing' even when track is null (shows 'open playlist'); copy is fine but consider hiding the '♬ now playing' header when off to reduce visual noise.

Suggestion: music-youtube/music-spotify hrefs are search URLs rather than canonical track URLs — fine for an MVP but if Prarthna wants deep-linked plays, update TRACKS[*].youtube/spotify to canonical IDs.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — every listed iter-3 behaviour verified live; no regressions

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run inserts 1 TEST_-prefixed guestbook entry + 1 contact message. Live e2e flow posted one additional contact message (name=TEST_iter3, email=iter3@test.com). Volume negligible.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live URL = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Desktop viewport 1920×900 reveals desktop-widgets sidebar (hidden below lg breakpoint). Boot ~3-4s then My Computer auto-opens once (autoOpenedRef guard). Window buttons (min/max/close) are now real <button>s outside the drag span — ordinary page.click() WORKS, no need for JS .click() workaround. data-testids of interest (verified live this run): desktop[data-theme=light|dark], boot-screen, icon-{computer,ai-investor,console,book,contact,music,dressup,bin}, window-{id}, window-title-{id}, window-drag-{id}, window-close-{id}, window-min-{id}, window-max-{id}, taskbar, taskbar-item-{id}, taskbar-clock, taskbar-theme (replaces old taskbar-sound), start-button, start-menu, start-{id}, start-resume, thinking-character, thought-bubble, desktop-widgets, widget-clock, widget-calendar, widget-sticky, widget-nowplaying, widget-stop, widget-open-music, music-play, music-stop, music-prev, music-next, music-youtube, music-spotify, music-track-{resilient|patient|hardworking|team-player} (NOT by index), cartridge-{ratcode,mnist,bookstore}, book-prev, book-next, book-page-{0,1,2}, investor-tip-next, investor-github-link, contact-{name,email,message,submit,success,error}, outfit-{y2k|tech|summer|arcade|denim} (NOT outfit-0..4 — update from iter2 context), mobile-tamagotchi, mobile-app-{id}, mobile-back. Theme persists via localStorage key 'prarthnaos-theme' ('light'|'dark').
rca_of_the_issue: Previous iter-2 pointer-capture bug RCA was correct: setPointerCapture on the entire titlebar swallowed synthetic clicks from automation on the inner min/max/close buttons. Iter-3 fix (verified): Window.jsx now has a dedicated drag-handle span (data-testid=window-drag-{id}) that owns the pointer handlers, and the three control buttons live in a sibling flex container with their own onPointerDown=stopPropagation + per-button onClick handlers. Result: page.click('[data-testid="window-close-{id}"]') consistently triggers onClose, which sets {closed: true}, which excludes the window from both the rendered list and the taskbar map. Verified by querySelector returning null for window-{id} and taskbar-item-{id} after each click.
